lines.en_lit: what does come As it was taught In what manner would do He place the fat over the place of slaughter, and bring it up and that is the respectful way toward the Most High,
MISHNA: the third lottery, new to the incense participate in the lottery The fourth new with old who take the limbs up from the ramp, to the altar.
GEMARA: taught No person ever performed twice, What is the reason Rabbi Ḥanina said: because it brings wealth

Rav Pappa said to Abaye: What is the reason If we say it is because it is written: “They shall put incense before You and it is written after that: “Bless, O Lord, his substance” if so, also a burnt-offering, since it is written “And whole burnt-offerings on Your altar.”
said to him: This, is frequent, and that, is infrequent.

Rava said: You do not find a young Torah scholar who gives instruction unless he comes from the tribe of Levi or from the tribe of Issachar. Levi as it is written: “They shall teach Jacob Your ordinances Issachar as it is written: “And of the children of Issachar, men that had understanding of the times, to know what Israel should do” And say Judah also, as it is written: “Judah is my lawgiver” I was speaking of those who draw conclusions according to the halakha.

Rabbi Yoḥanan said: They did not hold lottery daily afternoon offering. Rather, priest who won the morning wins evening, afternoon raises an objection Just as they hold a lottery in the morning, so too, they hold a lottery in the afternoon. When that was taught, to the incense,
But wasn’t it taught Just as they hold a lottery for it [lo] in the morning, so too, they hold a lottery for it [lo] in the afternoon. say: Lah,

But wasn’t it taught Just as they hold a lottery for it [lo] the morning, so too, they hold a lottery for it [lo] in the afternoon; and just as they hold a lottery for it [lah] in the morning, so too, they hold a lottery for it [lah] in the afternoon.
Rav Shmuel bar Rav Yitzḥak said: Here, we are dealing with Shabbat, since the priestly rotations are renewed
And to what we thought initially, there would be many lotteries, all would come in the morning, who would win the morning would win who would win the afternoon would win

The fourth new with old The mishna is not in accordance with Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov. As we learned Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov says: who takes the limbs up to the ramp is the one who takes them up to the altar.
With regard to what do they disagree? One Sage, holds “In the multitude of people is the king’s glory” And one Sage, holds not proper conduct the place of the Divine Presence
Rava said: Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov, is not of Rabbi Yehuda, And Rabbi Yehuda is not of Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov. if it would be so there would be too few lotteries;
And if you find a tanna who teaches five
